channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jason Kelce's wife, Kylie, announces she is pregnant with the couple's fourth child
item.description: Former Philadelphia Eagles center Jason Kelce's wife announced Friday that she's pregnant with the couple's fourth child.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: After Trump's Project 2025 denials, he is tapping its authors and influencers for key roles
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As a former and potentially future president, Donald Trump hailed what would become Project 2025 as a road map for “exactly what our movement will do” with another crack at the White House.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A man called 911 for help during a home invasion. Las Vegas police fatally shot him
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A Las Vegas man called for police help during a home invasion before an officer shot and killed him, according to authorities and 911 calls. Brandon Durham called 911 early on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump taps Rollins as agriculture chief, completing proposed slate of Cabinet secretaries
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he will nominate former White House aide Brooke Rollins to be his agriculture secretary.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hyundai, Kia recall over 200K electric cars over power issues
item.description: Hyundai’s recall encompasses over 145,000 cars, including some IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 6 EVs along with Genesis GV60, Genesis GV70 and Genesis G80 models.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Canada's top military commander calls out US senator for questioning a woman's role in combat
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — The first woman to command Canada's military called out a U.S. senator on Saturday for questioning the role of women in combat. Gen. Jennie Carignan responded to comments made by Idaho Republican Sen. Jim Risch, the ranking member of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas education board approves optional Bible-infused curriculum for elementary schools
item.description: Texas’ education board voted Friday to allow Bible-infused teachings in elementary schools under optional new curriculum that could test boundaries between religion and public classrooms in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Fox 5

item.title: Glen Powell to star in latest hit: a look-alike competition
item.description: After an appearance in "Top Gun: Maverick" and leading roles in "Anyone but You," "Hitman" and "Twisters," Austin native and Hollywood A-lister Glen Powell is set to star in his latest smash hit: A look-alike competition.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the most heated Thanksgiving food fights
item.description: Thanksgiving dinner may be one of the most anticipated meals of the year for many people, but it is also one of the most debated. From the correct way to prepare the turkey to the best side dishes and desserts, here are the dishes worth fighting over this Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: NATO head and Trump meet in Florida for talks on global security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. President-elect Donald Trump and the head of NATO have met for talks on global security, the military alliance said Saturday. In a brief statement, NATO said Trump and its secretary general, Mark Rutte, met on Friday in Palm Beach, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officer kills pet dog mistaken for coyote, owner says it was unnecessary
item.description: An animal control officer shot and killed a pet dog in a Massachusetts town after mistaking it for a coyote in an incident that has the animal's owner fuming, but that local police are describing as a sad mix-up.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Driver in crash that killed 4 kids was allegedly photographing ‘Welcome to Florida’ sign
item.description: A New York man was extradited to Florida this month after he was accused of causing a deadly crash when he came to a near-stop on Interstate 95 to take photo of the "Welcome to Florida" sign.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WGN 9

item.title: US reels from rain and snow as second round of bad weather approaches for Thanksgiving week
item.description: The U.S. is reeling from snow and rain while preparing for another bout of bad weather ahead of Thanksgiving that could disrupt holiday travel.
item.dc:creator: Noah Berger And Olga R. Rodriguez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Voters rejected historic election reforms across the US, despite more than $100M push
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Two weeks before Election Day, activists from across the country gathered for an online rally heralding the historic number of state ballot initiatives seeking to change the way people vote.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Storm inundates Northern California with rain, heavy snow. Thousands remain in the dark in Seattle
item.description: HEALDSBURG, Calif. (AP) — Heavy downpours fell over much of Northern California on Friday, causing small landslides, overflowing a river and flooding some streets, including in parts of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: NOAH BERGER and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jelly Roll pays tribute to Oklahoma teen who died from fentanyl
item.description: The 19-year-old died from a fentanyl overdose in May 2023, while attending Cameron University. Rain, who thought she was taking a Xanax, turned out the pill was laced with fentanyl.
item.dc:creator: Mario Gonzalez/KFOR, KRON4

item.title: Cases of 'walking pneumonia' are rising across the US: Here's where it's worst
item.description: In October, the CDC wrote that since late spring, the number of infections caused by walking pneumonia has been increasing, especially among young children.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, Fox 5

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Twitch star rants against Israel, calls for another 9/11
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenTable names 2024's 'Top 100 Restaurants' in America
item.description: OpenTable's analysts based their findings on more than 14 million diner reviews, along with diner ratings, reservation demand, and percentage of five-star reviews, among other factors.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Prolific Mexican cartel leader allegedly faked his own death to live 'life of luxury' in California
item.description: A Mexican cartel leader, who peddled thousands of kilograms of illegal drugs to the U.S., was killed by a business associate for "lying" — or at least that's what the public was told.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: A kayaker faked his own death and fled overseas. Now he's telling how he did it
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer so he could abandon his wife and three children has been communicating with authorities daily from Eastern Europe, even telling them how he did it, but has not committed to returning home, a sheriff said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal Reserve's likely slowdown in rate cuts could disappoint borrowers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Just a few weeks ago, the path ahead for the Federal Reserve looked straightforward: With inflation cooling and the job market slowing, the Fed appeared on track to steadily cut interest rates.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia election workers seek more penalties against Giuliani, saying he continues to defame them
item.description: Two former Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against Rudy Giuliani asked a judge Wednesday to penalize him even further for continuing to falsely accuse them of tampering with ballots during the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court ruling stops Louisiana from requiring Ten Commandments in classrooms for now
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Louisiana's plan to make all of the state's public school classrooms post the Ten Commandments next year remains on hold under an order Wednesday by a federal appeals court in New Orleans. The 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man files lawsuit against Netflix over Tyson-Paul fight streaming issues
item.description: A class action lawsuit was filed against Netflix over alleged streaming issues during the fight between legendary brawler Mike Tyson and former YouTuber Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Delta to begin serving Shake Shack on select flights
item.description: Travelers soon will be able to enjoy Shake Shack's famous burgers on select Delta flights beginning next month, with plans to expand throughout the U.S. in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Susan Smith is denied parole 30 years after drowning 2 sons by rolling car into South Carolina lake
item.description: Susan Smith, the South Carolina mother convicted of killing her two sons by rolling her car into a lake in 1994 with the boys strapped in their seats inside, will ask a parole board for her freedom on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Climate change goosed hurricane wind strength by 18 mph since 2019, study says
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — Human-caused climate change made Atlantic hurricanes about 18 miles per hour (29 kilometers per hour) stronger in the last six years, a new scientific study found Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
